original: At 12:51 EDT, Apollo 11 returns to Earth after taking the first astronauts to the Moon and returning them safely
A team of international astronomers led by Georgina Dransfield from the University of Birmingham announces the discovery of a planet outside the solar system

19-year-old Hungarian swimmer Kristóf Milák breaksMichael Phelps' 10-year-old 200m butterfly world record at the World Championships in Gwangju, South Korea, swimming 1:50.73, 0.78s faster than Phelps
New York to San Francisco foot race ends after 2½ months; the winner is 60-year-old Monteverde
Seattle's John Montague pitches 6 2/3 innings of perfect relief, tying the two-game record of retiring 33 consecutive batsmen
American explorerHiram Binghamrediscovers Machu Picchu, the Lost City of the Incas
The world's first children's railway opens in Tbilisi, USSR
Marvin the Martian, Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Chuck Jones and Michael Maltese (Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series), first debuts in "Haredevil Hare"
John Naber is the first to swim 200 m backstroke in under 2 minutes
First bison born in Banff National Park, Alberta, Canada, in 140 years after being reintroduced
"Inside Out 2" surpasses "Frozen II" to become the highest-grossing animated film in history, making $1.46 billion worldwide
Frances Oldham Kelsey, Canadian-American FDA Director (stopped thalidomide use in the US), born in Cobble Hill, British Columbia (d. 2015)
Jigme Singye Wangchuk becomes King of Bhutan at 16
Taiwan declares a Typhoon Day, closing all schools and businesses as Typhoon Gaemi makes landfall in eastern Taiwan with winds of 240 kmh (150 mph)
118°F (48°C) recorded in Minden, Nebraska (state record)
International Lawn Tennis Association institutes 9-point tiebreak rule
Global warming is the fastest in 2,000 years and scientific consensus that humans are the cause is at 99%, according to three major reports published in journals "Nature" and "Nature Geoscience"
Wild monkey attacks at least 42 people, including in their homes, in Yamaguchi City, Japan, leading police to be armed with tranquilizer guns
Paavo Nurmiruns a world record in the 2 mile (8:59.6)
Singer-songwriter iconJoni Mitchellmakes a surprise concert appearance, joining Brandi Carlile at Newport Folk Festival in Mitchell's first public performance in 19 years
Alice Ball, African American chemist (developed treatment for leprosy), born in Seattle Washington (d. 1916)
Caterina Jarboro, African-American opera singer and 1st black female performer to sing on opera stage in US, born in Wilmington, North Carolina (d. 1986)
John William Finn, American US Navy sailor, awarded the 1st Medal of Honor of WWII for his actions during the attack on Pearl Harbor, born in Compton, California (d. 2010)
(Leo) Gallagher, American stand-up comic and watermelon smasher, born in Fort Bragg, North Carolina (d. 2022)[1]
Benjamin Bonneville leads the first wagon train across the Rocky Mountains through Wyoming's South Pass
MLB Cincinnati RedsPete Roseties NL hitting streak of 37 games
Philippine officials declare a “state of calamity” around Manila as Typhoon Gaemi/Carina causes massive flooding, displacing 600,000 people and sinking an oil tanker
William Clarkis willed the enslaved man York, who goes on to accompany him and play an important role in theLewis and ClarkExpedition
Pine Tar Game:George Brett's home run is disallowed against the Yankees (overturned)
Chief Dan George [Tes-wah-no], Canadian Indian author, poet and actor (Harry & Tonto, Little Big Man, Smith!), born in Vancouver, British Columbia (d. 1981)
Évian Championship Women's Golf, Évian Resort GC: Brooke Henderson of Canada edges American rookie Sophia Schubert by one stroke to win her second major title
Citizens of Leeuwarden, Netherlands, rebel against ban on foreign beer
Second attempt to circumnavigate the globe as seven ships departs Corunna headed by García Jofre de Loaísa on orders of King Charles I of Spain for the Spice Islands (only one will make it)
HMS Beagle departs Maldonado, Uruguay
Olympics defeat St. George 25-17 at St. George's Cricket Grounds in Philadelphia in the first baseball game played in an enclosed field
First ptarmigan hatched and reared in captivity in Ithaca, New York
121°F (49°C) recorded near Alton, Kansas (state record)
V-2/WAC Corporal rocket launch; first launch from Cape Canaveral
112°F (44°C) recorded in Louisville, Georgia (state record)
Ugandan-English runner Deo Kato (36) begins trek from the Long March to Freedom monument in Cape Town, South Africa, to London, England to highlight the story of human migration
Mary, Queen of Scots, is forced to abdicate; her 1-year-old son becomes KingJames VIof Scotland
Tamla/Motown Records releases "Fulfillingness' First Finale," the 17th studio album byStevie Wonder; the album tops both pop and soul charts and includes the hit single "Boogie On Reggae Woman"
David Ortiz,Gil Hodges, Bud Fowler, Jim Kaat, Minnie Miñoso, and Buck O’Neil are inducted into Baseball Hall of Fame, Cooperstown, NY
Pope Francisarrives in Edmonton, Canada, to begin a penitential trip to meet with and apologize to First Nations, Métis, and Inuit communities for their treatment at church-run residential schools
Adolphe Adam, French composer (Giselle; Le corsaire; "Minuit, chrétiens!" / "O Holy Night"), born in Paris (d. 1856)
Ira Aldridge, famed 19th century African American Shakespearian actor (Othello), born in New York City (d. 1867)[1]
Alphonse Mucha, Czech Nouveau painter and artist, born in Ivančice, Margraviate of Moravia, Austrian Empire (now Czech Republic) (d. 1939)
Gloria George, Canadian Indigenous politician and activist (first woman to lead major Indigenous political organization - Native Council of Canada), born in Hubert, British Columbia[1]
Lynda Carter, American actress (Wonder Woman, Sky High) and Miss USA (1973), born in Phoenix, Arizona
French trader and soldier Antoine de la Mothe Cadillac founds trading post at Fort Pontchartrain; later becomes the city of Detroit
First trans-US rail service begins
American Johnny Hayes wins the London Olympic marathon in a Games record time of 2:55:18.4 after Dorando Pietri of Italy is disqualified for receiving assistance before the finish line
Heavy rain causes a mudslide that destroys a bridge at Nagasaki, Japan, killing 299
Single "Eye of the Tiger" by Survivor from "Rocky III" soundtrack starts a 6-week run at #1 on US charts (Grammy for Best Rock Performance)
American Western film "High Noon" is released, directed by Fred Zinnemann and starringGary Cooper,Grace Kellyand Thomas Mitchell
Kenneth Bancroft Clark, American civil rights activist (Dark Ghetto) and first black president of the American Psychological Association, born in Panama Canal Zone (d. 2005)
James Eric Storrar, English pilot (WWII - Operation Mosquito, destruction of Hitler's 'Eagle's Nest') and veterinarian, born in Ormskirk, England (d. 1995)
Lynval Golding, Jamaican-born British new wave rock and ska guitarist (The Specials; Fun Boy Three), born in Saint Catherine, Colony of Jamaica
Expedition to establish French colony in Louisiana leaves La Rochelle with four ships led by René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle (the expedition is disastrous with only 15 survivors)
First commercial hydroelectric power plant in the world begins generating electricity in Grand Rapids, Michigan
